description: Multi-agent configuration for Ohio ORC Title 45 Policy Bot. Five specialized agents with an Orchestrator entry point. Configure agents in Foundry portal in the order listed — specialists first, Orchestrator last.

sources.name: ohio-revised-code
sources.description: Full text of Ohio Revised Code Title 45 (Motor Vehicles), crawled from codes.ohio.gov

agents.name: definitions-agent
agents.description: Retrieves and returns verbatim statutory definitions from ORC Title 45 — primarily Chapter 4501. Do NOT paraphrase. Temperature=0 for deterministic output.

agents.name: traffic-violations-agent
agents.description: Answers questions about traffic laws, OVI, penalties, fines, and license suspensions under ORC Chapter 4511. Temperature=0 for deterministic penalty reporting.

agents.name: licensing-agent
agents.description: Handles procedural questions about vehicle registration (Chapter 4503) and driver licensing (Chapter 4507). Uses gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency on high-volume procedural queries.

agents.name: legal-reasoning-agent
agents.description: Handles complex multi-section analysis and conditional legal questions across ORC Title 45. Uses o3-mini reasoning model. Temperature MUST be 1 (required for reasoning models).

agents.name: orchestrator
agents.description: Primary entry point. Classifies user questions and routes to the appropriate specialist agent. Synthesizes specialist responses into a final reply. Create this agent LAST in the portal — it must connect to the four specialists as tools.

connectedAgents.description: Look up a verbatim statutory definition from ORC Title 45
Answer questions about traffic violations, OVI, penalties, fines, or license suspension
Answer procedural questions about vehicle registration or driver licensing
Reason through complex multi-section or conditional legal questions
